tokens: And when you get ready to drive off there, we'd like...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I guess we're a little late...(Stops to listen)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (We'd) like the rear Drive Power to Bus Baker and the steering to Bus Baker; Rear Steering.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: You're not in, you're...There you go.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I didn't think I was in.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay; Bus Baker with the Rear Drive Power And Rear Steering to Bus Baker.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Again, no impression of - to me, anyway - of layering or of bedrock, just loose rocks in the walls.
And they're splayed out in ray patterns.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Man, that (North Ray Crater) is some hole.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And there's about 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 rays coming out of there, intermixed red and...(correcting himself) white, and black rocks in each of the rays, and that's in about, maybe, a half of the crater.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: ...now, will be right down your tracks about a half a kilometer, and we'd like to stop in the midst of those big boulders you described on the way up.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: How far...See them back over there?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Looks to me like we just go around in a circle here, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Right down the same way we came.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We can't see old Orion from here.
(Laughing) This is going to be something going down this hill.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Sure we...We didn't...I'm not sure we came up that hill.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We sure come off it, didn't we?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Be sure that you got the brakes on.
Tony, this is at least a 15-degree slope we're going down, and that Rover came right up it and you never even knew it.
I should have had the (TV) camera pointed forward.
(Pause) Okay, Tony, that was at...I think it was 179 at 4.4, that little steep slope there.
Whoever said this was the Cayley Plain?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, that was down the rim of the crater here.
(To Tony) We've just set a new world's speed record, Houston; 17 kilometers an hour on the Moon.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Well, let's not set any more.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I got us out 2 hours and...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I guess that would be a new Moon speed record, wouldn't it?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Going back across Sun (and looking at) the (uphill) tracks, we just barely penetrated the regolith; maybe an eighth of an inch or so.
(Pause) Whatever it is, it's going to be real firm here.
Hopefully, we can get...Isn't (Station) 13 a double-core one?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To John) Feel that old Sun coming down on your...(Stops to listen)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I think we might do it there.
Here, I don't think...(Stops to listen)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You want to go...We'll go up on this ridge here, Charlie, because that's where the big blocks were.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That big one we thought was the rim and it was...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: What we're up on now is a sort of a pre-rim rim of this (North Ray) impact crater.
(Pause) And it's 600 meters from the rim.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'm panning your camera around at various places here - (with) the 16 - to get right and left.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It's probably out of film now.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Again, the impression is that...(Stops to listen) Already?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Turn it around this way, Charlie, and I'll tell you.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Man, that's hard on the old fingers.
(It feels) like it's still running, too.
(Pause) That's the Rover I hear...Feeling that thing vibrating.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Charlie, here we go again (down a steep slope).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, we just topped a...(To John) There's that...No, it can't be that.
There's a big rock over there; but this other one is down at the bottom of this hill here, John, (and it's the one) we came by.
(To Tony) We just topped another rise, and we're looking to the southeast back towards the Kant Plateau.
It's an undulating surface, and indented for about...It's difficult to judge distances.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Here's the big rocks down here, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (To Tony) But it's undulating until it hits the scarp of...I think I'll call it the Kant Plateau.
That's the scarp on the map that you can map around like a little re-entrant on the map back to the east, and we can see back up that way and all the way up onto the top of the plateau.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, and we won't want a Nav update of Station 13, so you can park either north or south, whichever is easiest for you.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (To John) We almost spun out on that one, babe.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay; let's go by that...See that big rock over there?
Maybe that's a permanently shadowed one.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I don't think so, but we can go look.
You know, following our tracks back, we might find that CSVC.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's right, that's what I'm thinking.
(Pause) If you see something that looks strange (meaning "alien") around here, that's it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: On down this ridge, we're going down about at least a 5-degree slope.

We have one real (meaning "very") filleted rock that we're just passing now at 3.8 at 183, and then we have another rock down here that's the same size, about 3 meters across that has hardly any fillet.
And that's the one we're going to stop by.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Is that what you meant, John?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To John) I don't think we'll be able to align it here at...I haven't had any trouble aligning that thing, though, in just about any position.
If we park north, we'll be in good shape for them.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause) Be able to see that biggie.
That rock right there looks like that great big one we sampled up on the rim, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I just don't think it's going to be permanently shadowed, though.
transcript.speaker: Duke
